source.definition: to begin living in a new house or apartment

explanation.sourceText: The phrase 'move in' combines the verb 'move', which comes from the Latin 'movere' meaning 'to move', with the preposition 'in', indicating entering or starting residence.
explanation.targetText: Expresia engleză „move in“ combină verbul „move“, care provine din latinul „movere“ însemnând „a mișca“, cu prepoziția „in“, indicând intrarea sau începerea locuirii.

source.additional: Regular verb phrase. Commonly used when referring to starting to live in a new home or room.

target.definition: a intra într-o locuință sau o casă

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb „a muta“ derives from the Latin „movere“, meaning „to move“. It is used to describe changing residence to a new home.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a muta“ derivă din latinescul „movere“, care înseamnă „a mișca“, fiind folosit pentru a exprima schimbarea locuinței.

target.additional: Regular verb of the 1st conjugation (-a). Mainly used in everyday language to describe moving house.

explanation.sourceText: The verb describes moving into a new apartment.
explanation.targetText: Verbul descrie mutarea într-un nou apartament.

examples.sourceText: I have moved into the new apartment.

explanation.sourceText: The word is also used to describe entering a new room.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul este folosit și pentru a descrie intrarea într-o încăpere nouă.

examples.sourceText: I would move in immediately if the room were free.
examples.targetText: M-aș muta imediat, dacă ar fi liber.

examples.sourceText: I have moved into the new apartment.
I will move in next week.
I would move in immediately if the room were free.
examples.targetText: M-aș muta imediat, dacă ar fi liber.

learningTips.text: Remember 'move in' means starting to live somewhere new—think of 'move' as changing place and 'in' as entering your new home.
The Romanian verb 'a muta' sounds like 'mutate' in English, which means to change—so 'a muta' means to change your living place.
Think of the phrase 'Eu mă mut!' meaning 'I move!'—it's a simple way to remember 'a muta' relates to moving house.
Link 'move in' with 'muta' by focusing on the shared idea of moving and starting fresh in a new place.
